name: Resend API
what_it_does: Modern developer-first transactional email API with React Email support, clean SDK design, and a focus on simplicity and deliverability.
use_cases: Sending transactional emails from agent workflows with minimal setup
React Email template rendering and delivery
Password reset, verification, and notification emails
Email testing and preview in development
Simple email automation without legacy API complexity
not_for: Marketing bulk email at scale (SendGrid or Mailgun better)
High-volume sending requiring custom dedicated IPs
best_when: A developer-focused agent needs the simplest possible email API with clean SDKs and React Email support.
avoid_when: You need marketing campaign features, custom dedicated IPs, or very high volume sending.

auth.notes: API key in Authorization Bearer header. Keys from Resend dashboard. Very simple - perfect for agents.

pricing.notes: Generous free tier. No credit card required. One of the best free tiers for transactional email development.

performance.rate_limits: 10 requests/second on free, higher on paid

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Excellent error messages - clear, specific, with actionable guidance. Best documentation quality among email APIs.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Each API call sends a new email.
known_agent_gotchas: Domain verification required before sending (standard for all email APIs)
Rate limits on free tier are lower than SendGrid/Postmark
Newer service with less battle-testing than SendGrid/Mailgun at high volumes
Batch sending API is newer and may have fewer edge cases handled

security_score_components.security_notes: Simple API key auth. Domain verification required for sending. No granular scopes — API key is all-or-nothing. Young platform — security posture still maturing. SOC2 in progress.
